title: Billionaire Paolo Rocca Steps Down as Tenaris CEO After 24 Years
summary: Argentine-Italian billionaire Paolo Rocca is stepping down from the helm of steel-pipe maker Tenaris SA, but will remain as chairman as Gabriel Podskubka takes the role of chief executive officer.

title: Zillow Drops as Profit View Misses on Legal, Ad Costs
summary: Zillow Group Inc. shares dropped as much as 9% in post-market trading on Wednesday after the company forecast second-quarter profit that missed Wall Street expectations, citing legal and advertising expenses and a flat housing market.

title: Kraft Heinz Joins Reverse Yankee Boom to Fund Debt Buyback
summary: Kraft Heinz Foods Co is hitting Europe’s bond market for the first time in over a year to raise euro-currency debt that will help fund a buyback of dollar notes.

title: Shell Profit Climbs as Iran War Boosts Oil Price, Trading
summary: Shell Plc reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter earnings as the Iran war boosted trading profits and drove up energy prices, helping it to overcome a drop in oil and gas production from the conflict.

title: Stocks Build on Gains as Traders Await Iran Update: Markets Wrap
summary: US stock futures extended gains and Brent fell for a third day as traders waited for updates on a potential US-Iran peace deal that could reopen oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Oil Dips Below $100 as US and Iran Weigh Deal to Reopen Hormuz
summary: Oil slipped below $100 a barrel, extending an 8% slump in the previous session, as the US and Iran weighed a fresh push to end their war and reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: UK Construction Slump Gets Worse as Cost Pressures Intensify
summary: British builders posted the largest decline in output since November as the Iran war pushed up input prices and discouraged sales.

title: US Waits for Iran’s Peace Deal Response as Israel Hits Lebanon
summary: The US is waiting on Iran to respond to its proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end a war that’s killed thousands of people, with tensions still high and Israel striking Lebanon’s capital on Wednesday.

title: Saudi Wealth Fund to Sell First Dollar Bonds Since Iran War
summary: Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund is selling a multi-tranche US dollar bond, a sign Gulf issuers are returning to public markets after disruption tied to the Iran conflict.

title: Maersk CEO Sees Passing Higher Oil Shock Costs to Customers
summary: The chief executive officer of A.P. Moller-Maersk A/S said the oil shock caused by the Iran war will significantly raise costs this quarter and next, which the world’s No. 2 container carrier will seek to fully pass on to customers.

title: Copper Edges Higher as Traders Await Iran Reply to US Proposal
summary: Copper rose for a fourth day, rallying alongside stocks and other risk assets as Iran evaluates a fresh proposal from US President Donald Trump to end a conflict that’s nearing the 10-week mark.

title: Shell Profit Rises as Oil Volatility Boosts Trading Unit
summary: Shell's profit surged in the first quarter as the Iran war drove oil and gas prices higher and an increase in volatility boosted the energy giant's trading business. Adjusted net income rose to $6.92 billion, the London-based company said in a statement. Bloomberg's Mitchell Ferman has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How an American City Can Become a Manufacturing Hub | Odd Lots
summary: The residents of Allentown are still sore about that Billy Joel song. And while it's true the Pennsylvania city became synonymous with deindustrialization after the steel industry began its decline in the 1970s,  it should be known for more: In the 1950s, some of the first mass-produced transistors were made in the city, the precursor of today's semiconductors. The city is a unique logistics and e-commerce hub — it's a day's drive from nearly 40 percent of the US population. In today's episode, recorded in Madrid at the Citylab conference, we speak to Mayor Matthew Tuerk about the city's grand strategy for building back and sustaining its manufacturing base, implementing industrial policy on a local level, how rezoning has changed in the last decade, the political puzzle of data centers, recruiting companies to come to Allentown, de-risking the American supply chain, and our favorite new category of industry — weight-gaining industries. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: French Container Ship Makes Rare Crossing of Strait of Hormuz
summary: A French containership has emerged in the Arabian Sea after last being seen in the Persian Gulf, indicating a rare crossing of the Strait of Hormuz by a Western European vessel.

title: UBS Fined €6 Million by Monaco Over Repeated Dirty Money Failures
summary: UBS Group AG was fined €6 million ($7 million) in Monaco over weak anti-money laundering controls, including filing a report 253 days after a suspicious transaction.

title: Chipmaker Silex Microsystems Shares Soar in Stockholm Debut
summary: Silex Microsystems AB surged in its first day of trading on Nasdaq Stockholm after an initial public offering that raised about 2 billion Swedish kronor ($220 million) for the specialist microchip-maker and its Chinese shareholder Sai MicroElectronics Inc.

title: PB Fintech Sees 30% Growth as India Insurance Expand

title: Philippine Peso Poised for Biggest Gain in a Month on Oil Relief
summary: The Philippine peso is headed for its best gain in a month as optimism over a US-Iran peace deal boosts prospects for further declines in oil prices.

title: Jet Fuel Concerns Hang Over Europe as Summer Vacations Approach
summary: In a matter of weeks, Europeans will begin heading off on their summer vacations — whether or not there will be enough jet fuel to transport them all remains an open question.

title: Gulftainer: Need Reliable Continuity And Resilience
summary: Farid Belbouab, Group CEO of global port and logistics operator Gulftainer, highlights how cargo is continuing to move through alternative corridors at a time when supply-chain resilience has become critical. He explains that Gulftainer is scaling from about 2,000 containers per week to 50,000. Belbouab speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Saudi Airline Faces Difficult Launch as Iran War Upends Travel

title: Intrum to Raise $812 Million of Equity Capital to Trim Debt

title: Earnings Beats in Europe Mask Tougher Times Ahead for Stocks
summary: A strong earnings season is hiding tougher times ahead for European stocks as the effects of the Iran war make it harder for companies to meet lofty profit expectations in the quarters to come.

title: Botched US Blacklist Highlights Frail Trump-Xi Truce
summary: As President Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, some long-standing tensions remain. Among them are the Pentagon's curbs on Chinese companies that could pose national security risks. Bloomberg's Kate O'Keeffe explains why this blacklist matters.  (Source: Bloomberg)
